error.signature: SnapshotRestoreException: [repo:snapshot/xxxxx] cannot restore index [my_index] because an open index with same name already exists in the cluster

verdict.summary: Snapshot restore failed. Index name conflicts, incompatible versions, or repository access issues.

dead_ends.action: Delete the existing index to make room for restore
dead_ends.why_fails: Deleting the live index before restore is confirmed causes data loss if restore also fails. Rename instead.

dead_ends.action: Restore from a different snapshot repository format
dead_ends.why_fails: Repositories are format-specific. An S3 snapshot cannot be restored from a local fs repo path and vice versa.

workarounds.action: Restore to a different index name using rename_pattern
Close the existing index before restoring (if acceptable)
workarounds.how: POST /my_index/_close  # then restore; the closed index will be replaced
GET _snapshot/repo/snap/_status  # check state=SUCCESS; also verify repository is accessible
